behaviors.description: Image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published
behaviors.justification: The real failure is when the actual vision model fails to detect explicit content in images. Mocking the model to return correct classifications hides this core risk. You must call the real vision model API against real test images to verify it actually detects explicit content correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real vision model API with test images known to contain explicit content, verify the model returns explicit classification, verify the post is held for review and not published to public feed.

behaviors.description: Text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques like letter substitution and unicode lookalikes
behaviors.justification: This is a robustness test against adversarial inputs. The real failure is the model being blind to obfuscation tactics. Encoding all obfuscation variants into a mock defeats the test's purpose. Only calling the real text model against actual obfuscation patterns reveals if the model is truly robust to evasion techniques.
behaviors.test_description: Submit text posts with known obfuscation techniques (letter substitution l33t, unicode lookalikes, spacing manipulation) to the real text classification model, verify each variant is still flagged as hate speech, verify posts are held for review.

behaviors.description: Post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped
behaviors.justification: This tests the decision-logic wiring and state routing. The real failure is in the orchestration logic (if image_safe AND text_safe then publish), not in LLM accuracy. Failures are: silent drops, incorrect state transitions, or routing to hold instead of publish. These are local bugs in the decision tree and state management that can be caught with mocked safe classifications.
behaviors.test_description: Mock both vision and text models to return safe classifications, submit a post, verify it is published (appears in public feed or published state), verify it is NOT held for review, verify no silent drops (post is neither lost nor queued).

behaviors.description: Full moderation pipeline—image check, text check, publish decision—completes within 5 seconds of submission
behaviors.justification: Mocking models eliminates real latency. Real vision model inference (multimodal APIs typically take 1-3 seconds) and real text model inference dominate the critical path. The 5-second SLA cannot be validated against mocks; you must measure actual end-to-end latency with real model calls to catch timeout failures and observe realistic timing variance.
behaviors.test_description: Submit posts with images and text through the real moderation pipeline (real vision and text model APIs, real infrastructure), measure wall-clock latency from submission to completion decision, verify latency ≤ 5 seconds, repeat 10+ times to validate consistency and detect latency outliers.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved
behaviors.justification: The real failure is how the system handles actual service unavailability (HTTP 503, connection timeout, socket reset). Mocking a service-unavailable exception hides real network behavior: connection timeouts, incomplete reads, SSL errors, and retry backoff interactions. Testing requires real HTTP calls (or real test-environment HTTP) to expose failure modes in actual network handling.
behaviors.test_description: With the vision model API endpoint returning 503 Service Unavailable (or network timeout), submit an image post, verify the post is NOT auto-approved, verify it is queued for retry, verify retry mechanism successfully processes the post once the service recovers.

behaviors.description: Moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window
behaviors.justification: The real failure is that LLM classification of borderline content exhibits non-determinism—the same image may be classified differently on subsequent runs due to model sampling and randomness. Mocking with deterministic returns hides this non-determinism. To verify the system enforces consistency despite LLM variability (via caching, result locking, or hashing), you must call the real model multiple times and observe if decisions converge or diverge.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image 5 times within a 30-second window through the real moderation pipeline, capture each classification decision, verify all 5 decisions are identical (all flagged OR all safe), verify the system's consistency mechanism prevents conflicting decisions even if the underlying model calls exhibit variance.
